title: Fei-Fei Li — Grokipedia
excerpts:
Section Title: Fei-Fei Li > Awards, Honors, and Publications > Key Publications and Books
Content:
<sup>[<sup>[[103]]()</sup>]()</sup> An earlier influential work, the 2006 paper "Learning Generative Visual Models from Few Training Examples: An Incremental Bayesian Approach Tested on 101 Object Categories," co-authored with Rob Fergus and Pietro Perona , advanced object category learning using Bayesian incremental methods on sparse data, demonstrating recognition accuracy on diverse real-world images and influencing generative approaches in vision. <sup>[<sup>[[104]]()</sup>]()</sup> In book form, Li authored the 2023 memoir *The Worlds I See: Curiosity, Exploration, and Discovery at the Dawn of AI* , published on November 7 by Moment of Lift Books, which interweaves her technical trajectory in vision AI with reflections on ethical and societal dimensions of the field.

title: Fei-fei Li | Professor of Computer Science, Stanford
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > Fei-fei Li
Content:
Professor of Computer Science, Stanford University
Fei-Fei Li is a Professor of Computer Science at Stanford University and Co-Director of the Stanford Human-Centered AI Institute. She is globally recognized for her pioneering work in computer vision, particularly as the creator of ImageNet, which helped catalyze the deep learning revolution. Li has served as Chief Scientist of AI/ML at Google Cloud, shaping its AI research and product strategy. Her research focuses on AI that augments human capabilities while aligning with ethical principles. She is a strong advocate for diversity in AI and co-founded AI4ALL, a nonprofit that expands access to AI education for underrepresented groups. Her work bridges cutting-edge research with societal impact, influencing global AI policy discussions.
